questions.q: In which Indian city is Mehrangarh Fort located?
questions.explanation: Mehrangarh Fort towers over Jodhpur, the second-largest city in Rajasthan.

questions.q: Mehrangarh Fort is located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Jodhpur, where Mehrangarh Fort stands, is in the state of Rajasthan.

questions.q: Who founded Mehrangarh Fort?
questions.explanation: The Rathore ruler Rao Jodha founded Mehrangarh Fort, which gave its name to the city of Jodhpur.

questions.q: In which year was Mehrangarh Fort founded?
questions.explanation: Rao Jodha founded the fort in 1459 when he shifted his capital from Mandore.

questions.q: Jodhpur, the city below Mehrangarh Fort, is popularly known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: Jodhpur is called the Blue City because of the blue-painted houses in its old quarter, seen from the fort.

questions.q: Approximately how high above the surrounding city does Mehrangarh Fort rise?
questions.explanation: The fort stands roughly 120 metres above the city of Jodhpur atop a steep rocky hill.

questions.q: Mehrangarh Fort was the stronghold of which Rajput clan?
questions.explanation: Mehrangarh was the seat of the Rathore Rajputs who ruled the kingdom of Marwar.

questions.q: Mehrangarh Fort is best described as which type of structure?
options: A hilltop fort and palace complex
questions.explanation: Mehrangarh is a massive hilltop fort that also contains ornately carved royal palaces.

questions.q: The kingdom ruled from Mehrangarh Fort was historically called what?
questions.explanation: Jodhpur was the capital of the kingdom of Marwar, ruled by the Rathore dynasty.

questions.q: What is housed within Mehrangarh Fort today for visitors?
questions.explanation: Mehrangarh now contains a well-regarded museum displaying royal artefacts, palanquins, and arms.

questions.q: From which earlier capital did Rao Jodha move his seat when he founded Mehrangarh?
questions.explanation: Rao Jodha shifted the Marwar capital from the older site of Mandore to the more defensible Mehrangarh hill.

questions.q: The rocky hill on which Mehrangarh stands is traditionally known by what name?
questions.explanation: The hill is called Bhakurcheeria, meaning the Mountain of Birds, in local tradition.

questions.q: Which famous courtyard palace within Mehrangarh was used for royal coronations?
questions.explanation: The Shringar Chowki holds the marble coronation throne where Jodhpur's rulers were enthroned.

questions.q: Which lavishly decorated hall in Mehrangarh is known as the Hall of Flowers?
questions.explanation: Phool Mahal, the Hall of Flowers, was a richly gilded chamber used for royal entertainment.

questions.q: Which mirror-decorated chamber is found within Mehrangarh's palaces?
questions.explanation: The Sheesh Mahal, or Hall of Mirrors, is decorated with intricate mirrorwork inside the fort.

questions.q: Mehrangarh Fort is constructed mainly from which material?
questions.explanation: The fort is built largely of the local reddish-brown sandstone quarried from the hill itself.

questions.q: Which gate of Mehrangarh bears the handprints of royal widows who committed sati?
questions.explanation: The Loha Pol (Iron Gate) carries the sati handprints of queens of Maharaja Man Singh.

questions.q: The Jai Pol (Gate of Victory) at Mehrangarh was built to commemorate a victory by which ruler?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Man Singh built the Jai Pol to mark his victory over the armies of Jaipur and Bikaner.

questions.q: Which annual world music festival is famously hosted at Mehrangarh Fort?
questions.explanation: The Rajasthan International Folk Festival (RIFF) is held each year at Mehrangarh Fort.

questions.q: The museum and conservation work at Mehrangarh is managed by which trust?
questions.explanation: The Mehrangarh Museum Trust, set up by the Jodhpur royal family, runs the fort's museum and upkeep.

questions.q: Which Hollywood superhero film notably used Mehrangarh Fort as a filming location?
questions.explanation: The 2012 film The Dark Knight Rises filmed scenes at Mehrangarh Fort in Jodhpur.

questions.q: The name 'Mehrangarh' is generally said to derive from a word linked to which deity, reflecting the Rathores' claim of descent from the Sun?
questions.explanation: The name is linked to Mihir/Surya, the Sun, as the Rathores claimed Suryavanshi (solar) descent.

questions.q: Which 18th-century Maharaja of Jodhpur built Mehrangarh's lavish Phool Mahal?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Abhay Singh, who ruled Jodhpur from 1724 to 1749, built the gilded Phool Mahal as a hall of private audience.

questions.q: A small shrine inside Mehrangarh honours Cheeria Nathji, the lord of which creatures, the former hermit of the hill?
questions.explanation: Cheeria Nathji was the hermit lord of birds who lived on Bhakurcheeria before the fort was built.

questions.q: According to legend, to ensure good fortune for the fort a man named Rajiya Bhambi was buried alive in its foundations. What is commemorated for him?
options: A grant of land and house to his descendants
questions.explanation: By legend Rao Jodha promised that Rajiya's family would always be cared for, and his descendants were granted land.

questions.q: Which famous old cannon is among the artillery displayed on Mehrangarh's ramparts?
questions.explanation: The Kilkila is among the noted cannons positioned on Mehrangarh's ramparts; Dal Madal is at Bishnupur, Jaivana at Jaigarh, and Malik-e-Maidan at Bijapur.

questions.q: Mehrangarh's marble Shringar Chowki holds the coronation seat used since the time of which 13th-century ancestor-figure of the dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Rathore coronation tradition traces back to Rao Sheoji (Siyaji), the dynasty's early founder in Marwar.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor's forces occupied Jodhpur and Marwar for a period after the death of Maharaja Jaswant Singh in 1678?
questions.explanation: After Jaswant Singh's death, Aurangzeb seized Marwar, sparking the long Rajput War of independence.

questions.q: The palace apartments known as Takhat Vilas were built by which 19th-century Maharaja of Jodhpur?
questions.explanation: Takhat Vilas was the personal chamber of Maharaja Takhat Singh, who ruled Jodhpur in the mid-19th century.

questions.q: How many fortified gates (pols) must a visitor pass through along the winding approach into Mehrangarh Fort?
questions.explanation: The approach to Mehrangarh winds through a series of seven gates built by successive rulers.
